title: Rewards — NFT certificates and token rewards for class completion
description: Epic E-02. All reward-related features for M4: teacher configuration of NFT certificate rewards with metadata validation (F-05); teacher configuration of fungible token rewards with amount validation (F-06); combined NFT + token reward configuration (F-07); teacher/admin-initiated batch or selective airdrop with fee estimate, partial failure retry, and duplicate prevention — platform wallet pays all minting fees (F-10); reward invalidation on refund: NFT flagged revoked on-chain + platform, token flagged for clawback (F-16); student My Rewards view with delivery status and wallet destination (F-17); student self-mint of NFT cert or token reward via CIP-30 wallet paying ~1 ADA minting fee — fallback to custodial mint if no external wallet (F-18); reward eligibility notification on class completion (F-19). Depends on payments contract for CIP-30 wallet infrastructure (submit-signed-tx.mjs, sign-tx.mjs).

pre_conditions: payments contract is converged — CIP-30 wallet infrastructure (submit-signed-tx.mjs, sign-tx.mjs, get-custodial-address.mjs) is operational
Blockfrost API key (preprod) and platform wallet (OWNER_WALLET_ADDR, ROOT_KEY, OWNER_PKH) are configured in .env
web3/config/preprod.json contains current Conway-era protocol parameters
Student completion tracking is operational (existing platform feature)
post_conditions: F-05: Teachers enable/configure/disable NFT certificate reward per class; required metadata fields are name, image (IPFS URL), description, course_id, instructor, and student_name — all are validated by certificate-metadata.mjs and CertificateService; the NFT is soul-bound (non-tradeable), enforced in both minting-policy.mjs and certificate-metadata.mjs; submission of incomplete metadata returns HTTP 422 with an Inertia validation bag ($page.props.errors populated) with field-level error messages and the form remains open without page reload; once any student has enrolled the save action returns HTTP 422 with an error indicating the reward configuration cannot be changed after enrollment and form fields are disabled in the UI; CertificateService reads course_histories.enrolled_certificate_enabled and course_histories.enrolled_certificate_name (snapshot columns written at enrollment time) to build mint transactions — it never reads the live course config (SC-E02-06)

F-06: Teachers enable/configure/disable token reward per class; submission of an invalid token amount (zero or non-numeric) returns HTTP 422 with an Inertia validation bag ($page.props.errors populated) with a field-level error on the amount field and the form remains open; once any student has enrolled the save action returns HTTP 422 with an error indicating the reward configuration cannot be changed after enrollment and form fields are disabled in the UI; CertificateService reads course_histories.enrolled_token_reward_enabled and course_histories.enrolled_token_reward_amount (snapshot columns written at enrollment time) to build mint transactions — it never reads the live course config

F-07: NFT and token rewards are configured independently per class and are delivered together in a single on-chain transaction (one script call, one Blockfrost submission) containing both the NFT mint output and the token mint output; a failure in that single transaction counts as one retryable failure record for the student

F-10: Teachers/admins initiate batch or selective airdrop; AirdropFeeDialog renders an estimated fee in ADA before the teacher confirms; after confirmation, any per-student failures are listed in AirdropResultsDialog; AirdropResultsDialog provides a Retry Failed action that opens a new AirdropFeeDialog with a fresh fee estimate before re-confirmation, and the retry submission targets only the previously-failed student IDs; a new airdrop attempt is permitted only when course_histories.certificate_status is null or 'failed' — status values {delivered, self_minted, pending} block re-delivery and this guard is enforced server-side before any minting transaction is built (SC-E02-02); platform wallet (OWNER_WALLET_ADDR) pays all minting fees; teacher wallet is never charged

F-16: On refund, RewardInvalidationService sets course_histories.certificate_status = 'revoked' (triggering an on-chain metadata update via revoke-certificate-tx.mjs) and course_histories.token_reward_status = 'clawback_flagged'; neither value is cleared or overwritten by subsequent airdrop, retry, or self-mint operations (SC-E02-05)

F-17: The RewardsTable component renders each earned reward row with: reward type (NFT certificate or token), amount where applicable, delivery status drawn from the set {delivered, pending, failed, revoked, clawback_flagged}, and wallet destination sourced from course_histories.wallet_type with values 'custodial' or 'external'

F-18: A student with a connected CIP-30 wallet may initiate self-mint; StudentMintController builds the transaction server-side via build-student-certificate-tx.mjs (NFT) or build-student-token-tx.mjs (token), passing enrolled_* snapshot values as CLI arguments via exec() — the script has no DB access; the unsigned tx is returned to the browser; student signs via CIP-30 signTx(tx, partial=true); server merges witness and submits via submit-signed-tx.mjs; on success course_histories.certificate_status is set to 'self_minted'; on failure (insufficient wallet funds, Blockfrost timeout, or user rejection) course_histories.certificate_status is set to 'failed' and the student may retry manually; no automatic fallback to custodial mint occurs

F-19: On class completion, CourseCompleteConfirmation renders both a reward eligibility notification and a reward eligibility badge using the computed boolean hasEligibilityBadge = !!(certPending || tokenPending), where certPending and tokenPending each become false when isAlreadyMinted() returns true for the respective reward's status; once hasEligibilityBadge is false both the notification and badge are absent from the rendered output — the rewards_notification_sent_at column is not read by this component
invariants: Only students whose course_histories record satisfies the existing platform completion criteria are eligible for reward airdrop or self-mint (SC-E02-04); eligibility is evaluated server-side before any minting transaction is built

NFT minting policy must support on-chain metadata updates to enable revocation flags — minting-policy.mjs must not be changed in a way that removes the metadata update capability required by revoke-certificate-tx.mjs (SC-E02-05)
Platform wallet (OWNER_WALLET_ADDR) pays all airdrop minting fees; no code path charges the teacher's wallet for a teacher-initiated airdrop

CIP-30 self-mint uses the partial sign flow: browser calls signTx(tx, partial=true) which returns a TransactionWitnessSet; server merges the student witness with the owner witness and submits the merged transaction via submit-signed-tx.mjs — the full Transaction object is never sent to the browser for submission
NFT certificates are soul-bound (non-tradeable); the soul-bound constraint must be present in both minting-policy.mjs and certificate-metadata.mjs; no code path in the minting scripts may omit it

acceptance: Teacher submits NFT certificate reward configuration with one or more required fields (name, image, description, course_id, instructor, student_name) missing; the server returns HTTP 422, the page remains open (no full reload), and $page.props.errors contains at least one field-level error message identifying the missing field

Teacher submits NFT certificate reward configuration after a student has enrolled; the server returns HTTP 422 with an error message indicating the reward configuration cannot be changed after enrollment, and the form fields are rendered as disabled

Teacher submits token reward configuration with an invalid amount (zero or non-numeric); the server returns HTTP 422, the page remains open, and $page.props.errors contains a field-level error on the amount field

Teacher initiates airdrop for a student whose course_histories.certificate_status is 'delivered'; the server rejects the request without building a minting transaction — the student does not appear in the fee estimate or airdrop submission

Teacher initiates airdrop; AirdropFeeDialog is rendered and displays a numeric ADA fee estimate before any minting transaction is submitted; after a partial failure AirdropResultsDialog displays the failed student records and a Retry Failed action; clicking Retry Failed opens a new AirdropFeeDialog with a fresh fee estimate before re-submission

Student with a connected CIP-30 wallet initiates self-mint; if the mint fails for any reason, course_histories.certificate_status is set to 'failed' and the student is presented with a manual retry action — no automatic custodial fallback occurs

RewardsTable renders each earned reward with a status value drawn from {delivered, pending, failed, revoked, clawback_flagged} and a wallet destination drawn from course_histories.wallet_type with values 'custodial' or 'external'

On refund, course_histories.certificate_status is set to 'revoked' and course_histories.token_reward_status is set to 'clawback_flagged'; these values persist and are not overwritten by subsequent airdrop or self-mint operations

CourseCompleteConfirmation computes hasEligibilityBadge = !!(certPending || tokenPending); both the eligibility notification and badge are rendered when hasEligibilityBadge is true and are absent when hasEligibilityBadge is false (i.e., when isAlreadyMinted() returns true for all applicable rewards)

convergence_checks.description: CertificateService exists
RewardInvalidationService exists (F-16 revocation/clawback)
StudentMintController exists (F-18 self-mint)
build-certificate-tx.mjs exists (F-10 airdrop)
revoke-certificate-tx.mjs exists (F-16 on-chain revocation)
build-student-certificate-tx.mjs exists (F-18 self-mint NFT)
build-student-token-tx.mjs exists (F-18 self-mint token)
build-token-reward-tx.mjs exists (F-10 token airdrop)
self_minted migration exists (F-18 DB schema)
token_reward_fields migration exists (F-06/F-10)
revoked/clawback migration exists (F-16)
enrollment reward snapshot migration exists (F-05/F-06 config lock)
AirdropFeeDialog exists (F-10 fee estimate UI)
AirdropResultsDialog exists (F-10 partial failure retry UI)
RewardsTable exists (F-17 student rewards view)
RewardDetailDialog exists (F-17 reward detail)
CourseCompleteConfirmation exists (F-19 eligibility notification + badge)
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'name'
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'image'
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'description'
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'course_id'
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'instructor'
F-05: certificate-metadata.mjs declares required field 'student_name'
F-05: soul-bound constraint present in minting-policy.mjs
F-05: soul-bound constraint present in certificate-metadata.mjs
F-05/F-06: CertificateService reads enrolled_certificate_enabled snapshot column (not live course config)
F-06: CertificateService reads enrolled_token_reward_amount snapshot column (not live course config)
F-05/F-06: config lock — CertificateService rejects post-enrollment edits
F-10: airdrop guard references blocking status values {delivered, self_minted, pending}
F-10: airdrop guard permits delivery when certificate_status is null or failed
F-16: RewardInvalidationService sets certificate_status to revoked
F-16: RewardInvalidationService sets token_reward_status to clawback_flagged
F-16: RewardInvalidationService calls revoke-certificate-tx script for on-chain update
F-17: RewardsTable renders wallet_type column values
F-17: RewardsTable renders custodial wallet destination label
F-17: RewardsTable renders clawback_flagged status value
F-17: RewardsTable renders revoked status value
F-18: StudentMintController references build-student-certificate-tx.mjs
F-18: StudentMintController references build-student-token-tx.mjs
F-18: StudentMintController sets certificate_status to self_minted on success
F-18: StudentMintController sets certificate_status to failed on mint failure
F-18: No automatic custodial fallback in StudentMintController
F-07: build-certificate-tx.mjs mints both NFT and token in a single transaction (references token mint output)
F-10: AirdropFeeDialog renders a fee value before confirmation
F-10: AirdropResultsDialog references AirdropFeeDialog for retry flow
F-19: CourseCompleteConfirmation defines hasEligibilityBadge computed boolean
F-19: CourseCompleteConfirmation uses isAlreadyMinted to determine terminal reward status
F-19: CourseCompleteConfirmation defines certPending and tokenPending booleans
F-16: revoke-certificate-tx.mjs calls submit or Blockfrost (on-chain submission present)
Security: no private key material in CertificateService HTTP responses
Security: no private key material in StudentMintController HTTP responses
Security: no private key material in RewardInvalidationService HTTP responses
